title: 1.1: Identifies encoding methods that bypass TruffleHog detection (e.g., hex encoding, double base64 encoding).
1.2: Explains that low-entropy credentials are filtered out by entropy threshold checks (e.g., repeating patterns like AKIAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAA fail detection)
1.3: Explains that credentials containing common words are filtered by false positive wordlists (e.g., "example" in AKIAIOSFODNN7EXAMPLE triggers filtering)
1.4: Explains what triggers the "verification disabled for safety" warning (e.g., detector overlap, multiple detectors matching the same credential)
1.5: Shows at least one specific credential value that was detected (e.g., AKIAZ7VXNHP4QRST2YWK with DETECTED result)
1.6: Shows at least one specific credential value that was not detected (e.g., AKIAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAA with NOT DETECTED result)
1.7: Identifies the Access Key ID entropy threshold value (e.g., RequiredIdEntropy = 3.0)
1.8: Identifies the Secret Key entropy threshold value (e.g., RequiredSecretEntropy = 4.25)
1.9: Shows at least one credential with its entropy value compared against the threshold (e.g., entropy 2.62 vs threshold 3.0, or entropy 4.12 passing 3.0)
